key_insight: LASKO treats skill edits as Lie algebroid sections, using the anchor to separate visible Markdown changes from latent structure and the bracket to detect noncommuting edit interactions.
engineering_relevance: It offers a cheaper way to optimize prompts, rubrics, plans, and validators by screening edit compatibility in microseconds before paying for full LLM-based validation.
method: Model typed, anchored Markdown skills as the base category.
Represent edit policies as sections of a controlled Lie algebroid with anchor \u03c1.
Use Lie-bracket tests to flag order-sensitive or incompatible edit compositions before rollout.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary benchmark results including nearly 15× speedup on a causal extraction task versus brute-force validation with a DeepSeek V3.1 4-bit 671B-parameter model.
watch_item: The gains appear preliminary and depend on how faithfully the anchor and bracket approximations capture real edit effects across different skill templates and workflows.
paper_title: Agentic Skill Optimization over Lie Algebroids
